titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Edward V. Knight Papers
titlestmt.author: Alison Fulmer

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 17513338.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday December 7, 2024 at 03:00

did.abstract: This collection contains the correspondence, photographs, and personal papers of Edward Vinton Knight, Yale 1931. Of note is Knight's correspondence to his family dating from 1931-1933 when he worked in Changsha, China teaching English with the Yale-in-China Association (now the Yale-China Association). Correspondence also includes communications about Japan's hostile activities during the invasion of Manchuria in the early 1930s and later during the Second Sino-Japanese War. There is a significant amount of correspondence dating from 1937-1945 from Knight to his fiancée and later wife, Lois Hathaway Upton. Photographic materials include black and white prints of Knight and his Yale-in-China colleagues and students as well as images of the landscape, architecture, and people in China. Personal papers include Knight's miscellaneous print and manuscript materials.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Sylvia Knight and David H. Knight, 2022.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This collection is arranged in three series: I. Correspondence, 1931-1988; II. Photographs, 1931-1951, undated; III. Personal papers, 1931-2002.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Edward Vinton Knight (1910-2004) was born in New Haven, Connecticut on April 4, 1910 to parents Reverend Franklin Knight and Gertrude Mosher Knight. Knight graduated from Holyoke High School in 1927 and graduated from Yale University in 1931. Knight spent two years in Changsha, China (Summer 1931-Summer 1933) teaching English with the Yale-in-China Association. Knight moved to Salem, Massachusetts and married Lois Hathaway Upton on June 23, 1939. Knight later taught English and Latin at the Darrow School in New Lebanon, New York. He also worked as a technical writer and later a marketing specialist for General Electric in Pittsfield, Massachusetts. Knight retired in 1975, moving to Lenox, Massachusetts and finally to Richmond, Virginia, where he died on November 13, 2004.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Edward V. Knight Papers. Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

All folder titles have been transcribed or derived from existing folder titles or material descriptions. These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Beinecke Archival Description Unit Processing Manual.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection contains the correspondence, photographs, and personal papers of Edward Vinton Knight, Yale 1931. Of note is Knight's correspondence to his family dating from 1931-1933 when he worked in Changsha, China teaching English at the Yali Union Middle School, a boys' preparatory school, with the Yale-in-China Association (now the Yale-China Association). There is correspondence from Association administrators to Knight's family regarding his travels and his bout with typhoid. Administrators also communicated about Japan's hostile activities during the invasion of Manchuria in the early 1930s and later during the Second Sino-Japanese War. Correspondence also includes a description of Knight's travels on the Trans-Siberian Railroad to Europe.

There is a significant amount of correspondence dating from 1937-1945 from Knight to his fiancée and later wife, Lois Hathaway Upton. Correspondence dating from 1937-1939 pertains to the period before Edward and Lois were married when Edward lived in Salem, Massachusetts while recovering from appendicitis and in Boston where he volunteered at Hale House and searched for work. Lois attended nursing school in Salem and Boston, graduating in November 1938. Correspondence from 1944-1945 pertains to the period when Lois and their daughter Sylvia lived with Lois's family in Salem during a difficult pregnancy with their second child, David. At the time, Edward remained in Pittsfield, Massachusetts where he worked with General Electric. Letters between the couple include information about their personal lives, relationship, and work.

Photographic materials include black and white prints of Knight and his Yale-in-China colleagues and students as well as images of the landscape, architecture, and people in China. Personal papers include material relating to Knight's travels, the Yale-in-China Association, and his miscellaneous print and manuscript materials.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright and other associated proprietary rights for unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by Edward V. Knight was transferred to Yale University in 2022. These materials may be used for non-commercial purposes without seeking permission from Yale University as the copyright holder. For other uses of these materials, please contact mssa.assist@yale.edu.com. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains correspondence written by (outgoing) and received by (incoming) Edward V. Knight. The majority of Knight's correspondence was written to his wife Lois H. Knight, née Upton. Description for correspondence was transcribed or derived from existing folder titles.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged in two groupings as received, Outgoing and Incoming. Outgoing correspondence is arranged chronologically. Incoming correspondence is arranged alphabetically by surname.

did.unittitle: Yale in China letters- typhoid, delay in travel

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Incoming correspondence is arranged alphabetically by surname.

did.unittitle: Phil and Ruth Greene in Changsha

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains approximately 200 of Knight's black and white photographic prints taken in China, Japan, the United States and elsewhere during his travels. Descriptions of photographs were transcribed from existing folder titles or from captions associated with the photos. A few dozen photographs have handwritten captions on the back.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The name of the hospital is illegible in the caption written on the back of the photograph.

did.unittitle: Burton and Mary Rogers, Pine Mtn. School
Burton Rogers, Richard Weigle, and Edward V. Knight
E.V. Knight and friends on Mt. Fuji

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hand writing on the envelope indicates that the college is "co-ed".

did.unittitle: National, cultural, and built environment of China
Photos in USA before departure for China

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains a formal goup photograph of Knight with a Chinese boys' sports team.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains 91 photographs of Yale-in-China Association faculty and students as well as casual snapshots of people and places in China.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains a formal group photograph presumably of members of the Yale-in-China Association.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains material relating to Knight's travels in China, the Yale-China Association, and his miscellaneous print and manuscript materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains two programs for musical performances by the Changsha Woman's Club and the Fuh Siang Girls' School Student YWCA.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains a page clipped from The Spectator magazine. One side includes a review of the book Manchoukuo: Child of Conflict by K.K. Kawakami. The other side contains the essay Confounding the Astronomers by Evelyn Underhill.

did.unittitle: Edward V. Knight journal of quotes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder includes copies of the Yale-China Review and correspondence and reports from the Yale-China Association.
